name: hcportal_toggleKennelSong
description: Toggles whether a song is associated with a kennel. @publicKennelId is resolved to the internal HC.Kennel.id (@kennelId) before all KennelSongMap operations. If adding (@isInKennel=1), either creates a new KennelSongMap record or un-soft-deletes an existing one by setting Removed=0. If removing (@isInKennel=0), soft-deletes the mapping by setting Removed=1. @songId is passed as the paramString to the access token check function.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: user making the request. Validated as NOT NULL and exactly 16 bytes via DATALENGTH. Passed to CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN. @songId is passed as the paramString to the token check function.

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: The public kennel GUID used to look up the internal Kennel.id (@kennelId) at line 160, which is then used in all KennelSongMap operations (EXISTS check, UPDATE un-remove, INSERT, UPDATE remove). Validated as NOT NULL and exactly 16 bytes via DATALENGTH.

parameters.name: songId
parameters.description: The song ID to add/remove. Validated as NOT NULL and exactly 16 bytes via DATALENGTH. Also passed as the paramString to the access token check function.

parameters.name: isInKennel
parameters.description: Toggle flag: 1 = add song to kennel (or un-remove if already exists), 0 = remove song from kennel (soft-delete). Validated to be exactly 0 or 1.
parameters.typeMismatch: Parameter is SMALLINT, but KennelSongMap.Removed (which tracks this state) is also SMALLINT. No mismatch, but typically boolean flags should be BIT.

parameters.name: hcVersion
parameters.description: Client version string. Not used in SP logic (dead input).

parameters.name: hcBuild
parameters.description: Client build string. Not used in SP logic (dead input).

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message (COALESCEd to '<no IP address>' if NULL).

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Returned on validation or auth failures. Uses errorType codes 2 (validation) and 3 (auth). Validation errors do NOT short-circuit - if multiple validations fail, multiple error rowsets are returned before the final RETURN at line 152. The caller likely only reads the first rowset.

rowsets.name: SuccessResult
rowsets.description: Returned on success after adding/removing the song association. Only returned if all validation passes.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call (after validation, with IP data and error flag)
INSERT into HC.KennelSongMap when adding a song that doesn't have a mapping yet (uses resolved internal @kennelId)
UPDATE HC.KennelSongMap.Removed and updatedAt when adding a song that already has a mapping (un-soft-delete, uses resolved internal @kennelId)
UPDATE HC.KennelSongMap.Removed and updatedAt when removing a song (soft-delete, uses resolved internal @kennelId)

codeSmells.issue: FIXED (2026-03-15): All KennelSongMap operations previously used @publicKennelId directly instead of resolved internal @kennelId
codeSmells.detail: Original HC5 code used @publicKennelId directly in the EXISTS check, both UPDATEs, and the INSERT into HC.KennelSongMap. Since KennelSongMap.KennelId stores the internal HC.Kennel.id (not PublicKennelId), all operations silently matched zero rows — songs were never actually toggled. Fixed at line 159-160 by resolving @kennelId = ken.id FROM HC.Kennel WHERE PublicKennelId = @publicKennelId, then using @kennelId in: EXISTS (line 165), UPDATE un-remove (line 170), INSERT (line 176), and UPDATE remove (line 184).
SET NOCOUNT ON is present but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If the KennelSongMap INSERT or UPDATE fails (e.g. constraint violation), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.
The INSERT/UPDATE operations on KennelSongMap are not wrapped in a transaction. If the SP fails after modifying data, changes persist without rollback.

All validation blocks run regardless of prior failures. Each failed check SELECTs an error rowset but does not RETURN until line 152. If multiple validations fail, the caller receives multiple error rowsets.

Lines 32, 53, 74: Uses DATALENGTH(guid) != 16 to validate GUIDs. While technically correct (UNIQUEIDENTIFIERs are 16 bytes), this is an unusual pattern. Standard practice is to check IS NULL only, as SQL Server enforces UNIQUEIDENTIFIER format on input.
These parameters are accepted but never used in the SP body. They should be removed in HC6 or actually used if intended for logging.

Lines 169, 183: The SP manually sets updatedAt = @now. The table has a DEFAULT constraint that auto-updates this on modification. Manual setting bypasses the DB-level tracking and could cause confusion.
@isInKennel is SMALLINT but represents a boolean (0/1). Should be BIT for clarity. KennelSongMap.Removed is also SMALLINT instead of BIT.

Line 175: INSERT into KennelSongMap includes (id, SongId, KennelId, Removed, CreatedAt) but omits updatedAt, which has a DEFAULT. The table also has updatedAtBias, which gets its DEFAULT. Explicit is better than implicit for auditability.

KennelSongMap uses PascalCase column names (SongId, KennelId, Removed, CreatedAt) which is inconsistent with other tables using camelCase (createdAt, updatedAt). Not an SP bug but a schema inconsistency.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
